name: MistServer
description: MistServer — open-source full-featured streaming media toolkit (HLS, DASH, WebRTC)

seo.title: MistServer — Object Storage / Media API
seo.meta_description: MistServer: MistServer — open-source full-featured streaming media toolkit (HLS, DASH, WebRTC)

when_to_use: Open-source streaming server supporting HLS, DASH, and WebRTC
Self-hosted video distribution without CDN vendor lock-in
Low-latency ABR streaming for enterprise video deployments
SRT ingest and HLS output for broadcast contribution pipelines
Free streaming server for small media teams and broadcasters

pros: Open-source under AGPL with commercial option
Very broad protocol support (HLS, DASH, WebRTC, SRT)
Low resource footprint on modest hardware
Active development and Dutch team support
Good for academic and experimentation use
cons: Smaller community than nginx-rtmp or SRS
Support paid unless you go OSS-only
review.verdict: Capable OSS media server with enviable protocol coverage — watch the AGPL if you ship closed-source.
review.best_for: Streaming engineers wanting one server speaking every protocol on commodity hardware.
review.not_for: Teams unwilling to navigate AGPL or run their own infra.
